plain_definition: A technology-neutral Swiss statute, in force since 1 January 2020, setting conduct-of-business rules -- client segmentation, information and appropriateness/suitability duties, and organisational requirements -- for anyone providing financial services in Switzerland. FinSA contains no crypto-specific carve-out or licence category: dealing in and advising on crypto assets that qualify as financial instruments (such as FINMA-classified asset tokens) is subject to exactly the same conduct rules that apply to any other financial instrument, regardless of whether the provider also holds a FINMA licence.
formal_definition: Federal Act on Financial Services (Financial Services Act, FinSA/FIDLEG), SR 950.1, in force since 1 January 2020.
